propositions: Add a rebar.authorship.v1 attestation kind that lets an event be cryptographically signed by an author identity and verified against that identity's in-band public keys.

register_authorship_policy pins POLICY["rebar.authorship.v1"] to a sshsig-scheme policy and is invoked at attest package import so the scheme is selected from source-pinned policy, never the envelope.
sign_authorship checks ssh-keygen availability, builds a DSSE PAE and SSHSIG signature, and returns an envelope whose signature keyid is the author identity principal.
Trust root is always resolved from the identity entity's in-band keys via resolve_trust_root, and a round-trip signed by identity A verifies True against A.
An envelope signed by A must fail verification against a different identity B because neither the key nor the principal matches.
Tampering with the envelope payload or signature, or verifying against an unknown/keyless identity, yields a non-verified verdict without raising.
